human_verification.notes: Validated dispensary-execution boundaries, regulator-grounded age, patient, package, COA, POS, seed-to-sale, pickup, and delivery controls, validated packaging, and explicit specification-based runtime posture.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to dispensary execution readiness, lifecycle progression, exception handling, and completion evidence rather than generic retail service, clinical advice, legal interpretation, inventory administration, or security investigation.

Authority sources anchor the pack in California DCC retail, delivery, and packaging controls, Colorado MED retail and medical sales controls, and Massachusetts CCC retail, seed-to-sale, patient-access, and COA requirements.

Scenarios validate missing customer or package evidence, unsupported medical or purchase-limit requests, diversion-risk stops, held-inventory release refusals, conflicting local policy handling, and requests to mark work complete without evidence.

The package requires named cannabis records such as government-issued IDs, patient access documents, package tags, COAs, seed-to-sale transactions, manifests, and recall or quarantine evidence before the lane can act.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the package artifacts.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation results and readiness evidence.

notes: The specialist is validated as a spec pack and does not claim retained-implementation runtime behavior.

Promotion to a retained implementation should happen only after repeated production-like usage confirms stable retrieval of POS, seed-to-sale, credential, recall, and manifest records with low false-confidence escalation rates.
